question_text: Which of the following would increase the strength of an electromagnet?

options.label: A
options.text: Decreasing the number of coils in the solenoid

options.label: B
options.text: Increasing the electric current flowing through the wire

options.label: C
options.text: Removing the iron core from the coil

options.label: D
options.text: Using thinner wire in the solenoid

explanation: The strength of an electromagnet increases when the electric current increases. More current means a stronger magnetic field. Other factors that increase strength include adding more coils and keeping an iron core in place.
